title: Validate R1 Neo battery ADC on physical hardware
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-usqf can model R1 Neo VBAT on P0.31/AIN7 with Zephyr voltage-divider devicetree and simulated ADC tests, but actual divider routing, ratio accuracy, ADC calibration/noise, battery range, and board power behavior require a physical R1 Neo. What needs to be done: flash the R1 Neo gateway firmware, compare HAL-reported battery_voltage_mv against a trusted meter or bench supply across representative voltages, verify no PMIC/power-gpio behavior is missing, and adjust devicetree ratio/calibration if needed. Hardware required.
notes: Blocked on physical R1 Neo hardware. No-hardware parent project-LICHEN-usqf models VBAT as a Zephyr voltage-divider on P0.31/AIN7 and validates conversion via ADC emulator, but actual divider routing, multiplier accuracy, ADC calibration/noise, battery range, and board power behavior require flashing a physical R1 Neo and comparing HAL-reported battery_voltage_mv to a meter or bench supply.
